title: Keep each commit atomic and self-describing
description: Shape commits so each one is a single coherent change that builds and tests on its own, making review, revert, and bisect reliable.
body: Use when staging work for review or preparing a branch to merge. Do not bundle unrelated refactors, formatting, and behavior changes into one commit. Actions: stage hunks selectively so each commit is one logical change; write a message stating what and why; ensure each commit compiles so bisect stays meaningful. Validation signals include a history where any single commit can be reverted cleanly and bisect lands on a real culprit. Risk boundary: a commit mixing a fix with broad churn makes a later revert risky, so split it before pushing.
